body.title: [in_progress] Aider builder — first headless run against Hex on a self-contained task (the known-quantity control arm)
body.description:
**Problem:** Akien's steer (2026-07-06): sprint toward an aider builder we're happy with FIRST — aider is a known quantity (proven to make small local models edit). Before wrapping it as a rack device, prove aider completes a real coding task against OUR inference (Hex) on a self-contained sandbox, and see whether it edits where the DS pipeline produced 0 edits.

**Proposed shape:** Install the local aider checkout (/home/akien/dev/src/aider) into an ISOLATED venv (never UU's venv; no aider imports into unseen_university/). Create a throwaway sandbox git repo (own git, no UU remote — blast radius contained by construction) holding a self-contained capability task: a stub module + a FAILING pytest. Run aider headless (--message --yes) with OLLAMA_API_BASE=http://10.0.0.100:11434, --model ollama_chat/devstral-small-2:24b. Observe: does aider edit? do the tests pass? Repeat with qwen3-coder:30b. Capture the invocation as a reviewable script (the seed of the repeatable aider-builder runner).

**Affected files:** new sandbox + isolated venv (outside the repo); a reviewable runner script under devlab/claudecode/ or scripts/. NO changes to unseen_university/ (aider is external, subprocess-only).
**Design rules:** no aider imports into unseen_university/; Inference-Proxy-only does NOT apply to aider (it is an external control arm — its bytes get corpus-captured later via a Stage-2 proxy endpoint, separate ticket); no SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** first-run + observation + a reusable invocation script. Do NOT build the AiderDevice rack wrapper (follow-up) and do NOT wire the proxy endpoint here.
**Completion criteria:**
- aider runs headless against Hex and completes the sandbox task (edits applied, failing test flips to green) with at least one of {devstral-24b, qwen3-coder:30b}.
- The invocation is captured as a reviewable, re-runnable script.
- Result recorded: which model, did-it-edit, tests-green, wall-clock — the first harness-gap datapoint (aider vs the DS 0-edits baseline).
**Test plan:** the sandbox's own pytest IS the proof — RED (stub fails) before aider, GREEN (aider's edits pass) after. If aider fails to edit, close shipped-unproven naming the lever (routing/edit-format/model).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that aider — a known-quantity builder — is proven to make our local models edit on our own inference, giving us a working builder to run on the swarm NOW and a control-arm baseline that prices the DS harness gap. Serves the aider-builder-first standing intention and DS.0 leveling.
